title: Vetted-source allow-list + per-source license-verification protocol
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task operationalizes the editorial & safety policy's source-tiering rule into a concrete vetted-source allow-list and a per-source license-verification protocol that every later sourcing/drafting task is bound by. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Establish the vetted-body allow-list (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG) and define the per-source license-verification protocol and ledger fields that gate whether any source may back a shipped assertion.
acceptanceCriteria: Establishes the vetted-body allow-list (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG; additions only by editorial decision with oncologist concurrence)
Defines the per-source license-verification steps and ledger fields (body, citation, URL, retrieval date, verified license/reuse terms, lastVerified, validUntil)
Records the conservative defaults: NCI/PDQ government-work-but-non-endorsement/trademark; COG/ESMO/CCLG copyrighted -> restate-and-cite, no verbatim copying without recorded permission
States that a source with unverified or incompatible terms cannot back a shipped assertion
